overview.title: Account Overview
overview.content:
## California Department of Industrial Relations (DIR)

The Department of Industrial Relations (DIR) is a major California state agency responsible for protecting workers, improving working conditions, and administering workers' compensation. DIR oversees programs including workplace safety (Cal/OSHA), wage enforcement, apprenticeship standards, and workers' compensation adjudication.

**Servos Relationship:** Servos LLC is a ServiceNow consulting partner providing implementation, maintenance, and modernization services to DIR across multiple divisions and projects. The engagement spans several active workstreams including LETF/Public Works portal development, HRSD modernization, QME implementation, and EAMS Courts.

**Strategic Importance:** DIR represents approximately **50% of total Servos revenue** as of early 2026, with expected revenue of **$500K/month** consistently through the year. This makes DIR the single most critical client account in the Servos portfolio.

**Revenue Overview (California Contract Portfolio):**
- Current LETF M&O contract: ~$700K remaining to bill (early April 2026)
- New GAP contract (Mar 2026 – Oct 2027): ~$5M total
- QME project: $4.6M over 18 months (awarded)
- LETF Phase 2: $8M over 2 years — largest single submission in Servos history
- HR deal (via SLP): $2.6–3M over 1–1.5 years at ~$180–200/hour effective rate
- ServiceNow HR licensing dispute: $400K at stake (escalated to DGS level)

**Subcontracting Partner (Transitioning):** HF Tech Services (Allan Hart) currently serves as the primary subcontractor, handling day-to-day DIR project management, invoicing through DIR's purchase order system, and direct client coordination. Servos is transitioning to **direct state contracts** in April 2026, eliminating the HFTech middleman to improve margins and operational control.

**Teaming Partner (EAMS Courts):** KPMG is the prime contractor on the EAMS Courts RFP, with Servos as a subcontractor. Key KPMG contacts include Amanda Hutchinson, Allen Sheldon, Ashley Miller, and Audra Mehl.

**Strategic Partnerships:**
- **ValueCentrix** (Shilpa Jana) — Mutual NDA executed via DocuSign (April 2026). Introduction facilitated by Don Page.
- **Kate Bell Strategies** (Kate Bell) — California government relations and legislative intelligence, providing strategic advisory on budget hearings, CDT leadership changes, and HCAI opportunities.

contacts.title: Key Contacts
contacts.content:
## Servos Team

| Name | Role | Email |
|------|------|-------|
| **Will Loving** | CEO / Account Executive | wloving@servos.io |
| **Matt Miller** | Solutions Strategy / Delivery Lead | mmiller@servos.io |
| **Ashley Haglin** | Engagement Manager (on-site at DIR) | ahaglin@servos.io |
| **Don Page** | CA Business Development | dpage@servos.io |
| **Marnie Henry** | Contracts / Legal | mhenry@servos.io |
| **Corey Godfrey** | Technical Consultant (on-site at DIR) | cgodfrey@servos.io |
| **Molly Lanning** | Utilization / Resource Management | mlanning@servos.io |
| CalTeam | CA Team Distribution List | CalTeam@servos.io |

### Ashley Haglin — Engagement Manager
Ashley is currently managing delivery for the California account, which represents the largest single account at Servos. She is overseeing approximately **56% of all client work company-wide** in addition to managing all company delivery operations, making her the most senior person in the organization from a capability standpoint. She maintains strong direct relationships with **Suman Biswas** (DIR CIO) and **Priya Ayyagari** (platform lead). Given this bandwidth concentration, hiring a junior Engagement Manager for California is a near-term priority.

### Corey Godfrey — Technical Consultant
Corey is currently deeply embedded in LETF project management work, which has limited his ability to properly manage broader California account responsibilities. The planned hire of a junior EM for California is intended to free Corey to focus on technical delivery rather than account management overhead.

## HF Tech Services (Subcontractor — Transitioning)

| Name | Role | Email |
|------|------|-------|
| **Allan Hart** | Principal / Subcontractor PM | allan@hftechservices.com |

*Note: Servos currently holds only an MSA with Allan Hart — no direct state contract exists yet. SOWs with Allan are needed to cover California POs for the LETF Project, AB1121, and GAP contract during the transition period. Allan is expected to sign whatever documentation is required. Full transition to direct state contracts is planned for April 2026.*

timeline.title: Timeline of Engagement
timeline.content:
## Key Milestones

### 2025
- **Nov 2025**: Active DIR staffing — Ashley Haglin and Corey Godfrey embedded at DIR
- **Ongoing**: LETF/Public Works ServiceNow platform maintenance and operations
- **Ongoing**: AB1121 compliance project work

### 2026 Q1

**February 2026:**
- RFO 17392 (SLP) addendum discussions — Allan Hart and Matt Miller
- Servos LLC Subcontracting MSA fully executed (Case ID 04838)
- CA DIR EAMS Courts teaming agreement redlines exchanged between Servos and KPMG
- EAMS Partnership Paragraph and mutual NDA discussions
- RFO 17703 (QME Implementation Services) addendum 1 discussions
- Invoice 400-1475 for PO 7350-0000017327 submitted
- CUF — DIR LETF/PW Phase 2 & M&O Services RFP 17356 document signed
- Multiple project manager references submitted via DocuSign (HRSD, WSD, IRWORKS)

**March 2026:**
- **Mar 2**: RFO 17703 (QME) request for clarification
- **Mar 3**: EAMS Partnership Paragraph coordination with Grace Knight, Zori Boyechko
- **Mar 11**: Multiple invoices processing with DIR finance
- **Mar 12**: RFO #18047 HRSD major discussion thread — Don Page, Megan Mulvihill, Allan Hart
- **Mar 13**: Purchase Order 19184 received for QME Implementation Services
- **Mar 19-21**: CDT Director retirement announced — Kate Bell strategic discussions
- **Mar 21**: EAMS Courts RFP reviewed by Katie DeAngelis at CDT/CIO
- **Mar 24**: **EAMS Courts RFP response submitted** — team celebration
- **Mar 25-26**: HRSD RFO #18047 addendum work continues; HCAI opportunity identified
- **Mar 27**: LETF M&O, Extension, & AB1121 Project Status Report delivered to DIR
- **Mar 28-30**: Subcontractor work percentage confirmation; invoice processing
- **Mar 30**: DIR RFP 17356 LETF/PW clarification from CDT/CIO (Wendy Bright)

### 2026 Q2 (Current)

**April 2026:**
- **Apr 2**: AB1121 WOA4 & WOA7 update from Corey Godfrey
- **Apr 3-4**: **GO-LIVE: New Public Works Portal & DIR Services Portal Redesign** — Major milestone achieved. Team successfully resolved payment provider issues during implementation.
- **Apr 2026 (in progress)**: Transition to direct state contracts — eliminating HFTech subcontract layer for improved margins
- **Apr 2026 (in progress)**: SOWs with Allan Hart being prepared for LETF Project, AB1121, and GAP contract bridge period

scope.title: Active Projects & Scope of Work
scope.content:
## 1. LETF/Public Works — ServiceNow Platform
**Status: ACTIVE — JUST WENT LIVE (April 2026)**
**RFP 17356**

ServiceNow-based portal for the Labor Enforcement Task Force (LETF) and Public Works programs.

- Phase 2 development and Maintenance & Operations
- New Public Works Portal — **Go-Live April 3-4, 2026** (payment provider issues resolved during implementation)
- DIR Services Portal Redesign — delivered alongside Public Works
- CDT Oversight: Wendy Bright at CDT/CIO
- Subcontractor: HF Tech Services (Allan Hart)
- **Purchase Order**: PO 7350-0000016706
- **Invoices**: 400-1483, 400-1504
- **Remaining to bill**: ~$700K on current LETF contract (early April 2026)

## 2. GAP Contract
**Status: ACTIVE — New Contract**
**Period: March 2026 – October 2027**

- Total contract value: ~$5M
- Bridge/continuation contract covering ongoing DIR platform support
- Part of the transition to direct state contract structure

## 3. AB1121 Compliance
**Status: ACTIVE**

Legislative compliance project related to Assembly Bill 1121.

- Work Order Authorities: WOA4 and WOA7 (as of April 2026)
- Weekly project status reports to DIR stakeholders (Biswas, Godfrey, Ayyagari, Kim)
- Leads: Ashley Haglin (embedded at DIR), Corey Godfrey

## 4. HRSD — Human Resource Service Delivery
**Status: ACTIVE — Responding to RFO**
**RFO #18047**

- Active response to RFO #18047 with addendums being incorporated
- Team: Allan Hart (HF Tech), Matt Miller, Don Page, Megan Mulvihill
- Project Manager references submitted via DocuSign
- **HR deal (via SLP)**: $2.6–3M over 1–1.5 years at ~$180–200/hour effective rate
- **ServiceNow HR Licensing Dispute**: DIR wants to reduce from 2,700 licenses to 100 licenses until HR goes live — $400K at stake, escalated to DGS level

## 5. QME — Qualified Medical Examiner
**Status: ACTIVE — PO Received**
**RFO 17703 | PO 19184**

- Purchase Order 19184 received March 2026 for implementation services
- **Contract value**: $4.6M over 18 months (awarded)
- Team: Allan Hart (sub PM), Matt Miller (solutions), Marnie Henry (contracts)

## 6. LETF Phase 2
**Status: SUBMITTED — Awaiting Award**
**RFP 17356**

- **Contract value**: $8M over 2 years — largest single submission in Servos history
- Full platform Phase 2 development and continued M&O
- CDT Oversight: Wendy Bright at CDT/CIO

## 7. EAMS Courts — Electronic Adjudication Management System
**Status: SUBMITTED — Awaiting Decision**

- Servos is subcontractor to KPMG (prime)
- **Submitted March 24, 2026**
- CDT/CIO (Katie DeAngelis) reviewing
- Teaming agreement executed with KPMG
- Partner: DVB ETG (Richard McKinnon) also on team
- Originally specified ServiceNow, now open to any platform — **~50/50 chance of award** due to increased competition

## 8. Workers' Compensation Claims — New Opportunities
**Status: TRACKING — Two Separate RFPs**

Two distinct workers' compensation-related procurements are being tracked:

**8a. EAMS Courts Platform (WC adjudication):**
- Servos subcontractor to KPMG (see item 7 above)
- Originally ServiceNow-specific, now open to any platform
- ~50/50 probability of award given expanded competition

**8b. Claims Processing Platform:**
- State is moving away from Origami Risk for claims processing
- CIO is encouraging a ServiceNow-based replacement solution
- Servos is considering a prime response (not as sub)
- Significant opportunity if Servos leads the bid

## 9. ServiceNow SLP Partnership
**Status: ACTIVE**
**RFO 17392 | RFO 26-0224**

- Authorized ServiceNow SLP partner in California
- PS Master Agreement policy acknowledged
- Multiple RFOs flowing through SLP channel, including HR deal

## Purchase Orders & Invoicing

| PO Number | Project | Recent Invoices |
|-----------|---------|----------------|
| PO 7350-0000016706 | LETF/PW | 400-1483, 400-1504 |
| PO 7350-0000017327 | AB1121/LETF | 400-1459, 400-1467, 400-1475 |
| PO 19184 | QME Implementation | New — March 2026 |

deliverables.title: Key Deliverables & Outcomes
deliverables.content:
## Completed Deliverables

### Public Works Portal & DIR Services Portal Redesign
- **GO-LIVE: April 3-4, 2026**
- New Public Works Portal launched alongside DIR Services Portal Redesign
- Government Service Portals on ServiceNow platform
- Team successfully resolved payment provider issues during implementation
- Celebrated by ServiceNow partner team and Servos leadership

### EAMS Courts RFP Response
- **Submitted: March 24, 2026**
- Full RFP response submitted as KPMG subcontractor
- Teaming agreement fully executed
- Partnership paragraph and mutual NDA completed

### QME Implementation — Purchase Order
- **PO 19184 received: March 2026**
- Formal engagement for QME Implementation Services ($4.6M over 18 months)
- Followed RFO 17703 response and addendum process

### Ongoing Deliverables
- Weekly/bi-weekly project status reports (LETF M&O, Extension, AB1121)
- Regular invoicing through HF Tech Services to DIR

### References & Credentials Submitted

- IRS WSD Project Manager reference
- IRWORKS Project Manager reference
- Amanda Herrera CMDB, ITOM, HAM Project reference

status.title: Current Status & Open Items
status.content:
## Current Status (as of April 3, 2026)

### Active Now
- **Public Works Portal just went LIVE** (April 3-4) — monitoring and stabilization phase
- **AB1121 WOA4 & WOA7** — Active work with Corey Godfrey leading
- **GAP Contract** — New $5M contract (Mar 2026 – Oct 2027) active
- **Project status reporting** — Regular reports to DIR stakeholders
- **Invoice processing** — Multiple invoices in various stages with DIR finance
- **HFTech transition** — Direct state contract documentation in preparation

### Awaiting Decision
- **LETF Phase 2** (RFP 17356) — $8M over 2 years, largest Servos submission ever. Awaiting award.
- **EAMS Courts RFP** — Submitted March 24, under CDT/CIO review (Katie DeAngelis). ~50/50 odds.
- **RFO #18047 HRSD** — Response in progress, addendum 1 being incorporated

### New Opportunities Being Tracked
- **WC Claims Processing** — Servos considering prime response; state moving away from Origami Risk, CIO favoring ServiceNow
- **HCAI** (Health Care Access and Information) — Kate Bell flagged to CalTeam (March 26)
- **ServiceNow RFO 26-0224-1** — New ServiceNow opportunity through SLP channel
- **CDT Director transition** — Current director announced retirement; monitoring for impact on DIR projects

### Open Items Requiring Attention
1. HRSD RFO #18047 response completion and submission
2. LETF Phase 2 and EAMS Courts award decision monitoring
3. SOWs with Allan Hart — needed for LETF Project, AB1121, and GAP contract POs during transition
4. Direct state contract execution in April 2026 (eliminating HFTech layer)
5. ServiceNow HR licensing dispute resolution ($400K, escalated to DGS)
6. Hire junior Engagement Manager for California to free Corey Godfrey from account management
7. Subcontractor work percentage confirmation with Nathalie Esteron
8. DIR RFP 17356 LETF/PW clarification response to CDT (Wendy Bright)
9. ServiceNow SLP reseller authorization compliance
10. Post-go-live monitoring for Public Works and DIR Services portals
11. Invoice 400-1475 (PO 7350-0000017327) payment follow-up with Priya Ayyagari

technical.title: Technical Details
technical.content:
## Platform
- **Primary Platform**: ServiceNow
- **Partnership**: Servos is an authorized SLP (State & Local Program) partner
- **Deployment**: Government Service Portals on ServiceNow

## Key Systems

### ServiceNow Portals
- Public Works Portal (Government Service Portal) — **LIVE as of April 2026**
- DIR Services Portal (Redesigned) — **LIVE as of April 2026**
- LETF Portal — Active M&O

### EAMS (Electronic Adjudication Management System)
- Courts module — RFP for modernization (Servos sub to KPMG, submitted March 2026)
- Handles workers' compensation adjudication
- CDT/CIO oversight for procurement
- Originally ServiceNow-specific; now platform-agnostic

### QME (Qualified Medical Examiner)
- Implementation services on ServiceNow
- PO 19184 active, $4.6M over 18 months

### AB1121 Compliance
- Legislative compliance implementation
- Work Order Authorities (WOA4, WOA7) structure

### HRSD (Human Resource Service Delivery)
- ServiceNow HR module implementation
- Licensing dispute: DIR seeking to reduce from 2,700 to 100 licenses until go-live
- $400K licensing cost in dispute — escalated to DGS level

## SharePoint Document Repositories

Four SharePoint sites contain DIR project content:

### 1. CA DIR LETF M&O Services
**URL**: servosio.sharepoint.com/sites/CADIRLETFMOServices

- **A. LETF - General Project/**
  - *1. Contract Materials* (32 items) — WOA documents (WOA4–WOA11), PO 16706, RFO #15623, HF Tech Response
  - *2. PM Files* (19 items) — Schedule, Meeting Topics, Invoices, Status Reports, Roadmap, WBS
  - *3. Documents & Data* (15 items)
  - *4. Deliverables* (5 items) — Sprint showbacks, scope review checklist, eCPR search demo
- **B. User Story Collaboration** — Portal routing scenarios, DIR portal page notes, showback templates
- **C. Development Collaboration** — CDT Issue Response, IP addresses, development artifacts

### 2. CA DIR ServiceNow Platform Support
**URL**: servosio.sharepoint.com/sites/CADIRServiceNowPlatformSupport

- General Project — Contracts, PM Files, Documents, Deliverables
- User Story Collaboration — OEA Reports, facility work location mapping, ARB fields
- Development & Testing collaboration folders

### 3. California
**URL**: servosio.sharepoint.com/sites/California

- **DIR QME Phase 1** — Contracts, PM, Documents, Deliverables, Sales, QME Audit presentation, Go-Live Playbook
- **DIR Standards Board** — Contracts, PM Files, Documents, Deliverables

### 4. California Sales Team
**URL**: servosio.sharepoint.com/sites/CalTeam

- Team attachments and sales materials

## Contract & Procurement Structure

- **Current Structure**: HF Tech Services holds primary contract with DIR; Servos provides ServiceNow services as subcontractor
- **Transition (April 2026)**: Moving to direct state contracts, eliminating HFTech layer
- **EAMS Courts**: KPMG is prime, Servos is sub
- **Purchase Orders**: DIR uses formal PO system (7350-xxxx series and standalone POs)
- **Invoicing**: Through HF Tech to DIR finance (Amy Dickinson, Priya Ayyagari) until direct contract transition
- **CDT/CIO**: California Dept of Technology oversees major IT procurements

## California Government Context
- DIR reports through the Labor & Workforce Development Agency
- CDT/CIO provides IT procurement oversight for state agencies
- Budget hearings (Subcommittee #5) affect EDD and potentially DIR funding
- Legislative changes (AB1121) drive compliance requirements
- Governor's appointments affect agency leadership and priorities
- CDT Director announced retirement (March 2026) — monitoring for downstream procurement impact

## Key Acronyms
- **DIR** — Department of Industrial Relations
- **LETF** — Labor Enforcement Task Force
- **PW** — Public Works
- **EAMS** — Electronic Adjudication Management System
- **QME** — Qualified Medical Examiner
- **HRSD** — Human Resource Service Delivery
- **CDT** — California Department of Technology
- **CIO** — Chief Information Officer
- **SLP** — State & Local Program (ServiceNow)
- **RFO** — Request for Offer
- **RFP** — Request for Proposal
- **WOA** — Work Order Authority
- **HCAI** — Health Care Access and Information
- **M&O** — Maintenance and Operations
- **DGS** — Department of General Services
- **WC** — Workers' Compensation
- **GAP** — Gap/bridge contract (Mar 2026 – Oct 2027)
